description_long: This bungalow features a large, low-pitched hip roof covered in reddish-brown tiles, giving it a classic and sturdy appearance. The main structure is rectangular with a prominent, open framework extension on one side, likely representing a garage or carport. The exterior walls are predominantly dark, possibly glass or painted concrete, with large window openings that suggest an emphasis on natural light and open views. The roof overhangs the walls, providing shade and architectural interest. The building sits low to the ground, typical of bungalow designs, and the open framework extension contrasts with the solid main volume. The overall design is modern with clean lines and a functional layout.
description: A modern bungalow with a broad hip roof of reddish-brown tiles, dark exterior walls, and a prominent open framework extension, likely a garage or carport.
description_view_0: This view highlights the long side of the bungalow, showing the full expanse of the hip roof and the open framework extension on the left.
description_view_1: The front view displays the symmetrical hip roof and the main entrance area, with the open framework partially visible behind.
description_view_2: This side view mirrors view 0 but with the open framework extension on the right, emphasizing the roof's overhang and the building's depth.
description_view_3: The rear view shows the back of the bungalow and the open framework extension projecting outward, with minimal window openings visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with large grouped windows on main facades
